input.q:
Identify the most significant supply-chain disruptions and risks over roughly the last 12 months affecting the global glass packaging and glass-bottle supply chain.

Look across news, trade press and company announcements for events such as: energy-cost shocks affecting glass furnaces, plant or furnace closures, strikes, raw-material (soda ash, sand, cullet) shortages, freight and logistics disruruptions, and demand shifts.

For each event, return:
- What happened and when (with the date or period)
- The region and any named companies affected
- The impact on supply, capacity, lead times or price
- A source URL

Order the findings from most to least material, and note any recurring or structural risks the buyer should watch. Do not include events older than about 18 months unless they are still ongoing.

Sourcing and citation rules (apply to every claim):
- For each factual claim, cite the specific source that directly states that claim. Never attach a citation that does not itself contain the claim.
- Never cite a document dated before an event as the source for that event; the citation's own date must be consistent with the claim.
- Strongly prefer primary and authoritative sources: official company filings and press releases, regulators and government bodies, courts, standards bodies, and reputable news or trade press. Do not base a factual claim on SEO/marketing blogs, content farms, or open B2B marketplace listings; use such sources only if nothing better exists, and then explicitly label the claim as low-confidence.
- Label every figure as reported, estimate, or forecast, and give its date or as-of period.
- For any dramatic, contested, or fast-moving event, calibrate your language to what is actually confirmed, distinguish confirmed facts from uncertain ones, note where sources disagree on dates or numbers, and anchor to the most authoritative source available.
- If you cannot find a solid source for a claim, say so explicitly instead of asserting it.

output.answer:
**Executive Summary**

Over the past 18 months (early 2025 to mid-2026), the global glass packaging supply chain has been severely disrupted by compounding macroeconomic and geopolitical shocks. The most material event was the March 2026 West Asia crisis, which triggered a sudden suspension of Qatari liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, causing European gas prices to spike up to 54% and forcing manufacturers to implement emergency energy surcharges of up to 2,000% on freight. Concurrently, major glass producers—including O-I Glass, Ardagh, and Verallia—executed aggressive capacity rationalizations, permanently closing or idling dozens of furnaces across the US and Europe to manage subdued demand and high operating costs. These structural reductions, combined with volatile US-China-Mexico tariff policies and ongoing Red Sea/Strait of Hormuz logistics bottlenecks, have significantly extended lead times and increased landed costs for beverage and pharmaceutical brands.

---

### 1. Geopolitical Energy Shocks and Surcharges (March 2026 Crisis)
**Materiality: High** | *Impact: Severe price inflation, localized production curtailments*

The glass industry, which requires 24/7 furnace operations and typically attributes 10% to 20% of total manufacturing costs to energy, faced a severe shock in early 2026 [O-I GLASS, INC._December 31, 2024](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/812074/000155837025000868/oi-20241231x10k.htm). 
*   **The Catalyst:** On March 2, 2026, QatarEnergy suspended production of LNG following drone and missile attacks on its Ras Laffan and Mesaieed plants during the US-Israeli war on Iran [Soaring gas prices and disrupted supply chains will ripple out to increase costs in every store and sector of the economy – Darden Report Online](https://news.darden.virginia.edu/2026/03/27/soaring-gas-prices-and-disrupted-supply-chains-will-ripple-out-to-increase-costs-in-every-store-and-sector-of-the-economy). QatarEnergy declared force majeure on gas exports on March 4, 2026, and later extended it through early September 2026 for Italy's Edison SpA [Exclusive: Qatar shuts gas liquefaction, will take weeks to restart, sources say | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/qatarenergy-declares-force-majeure-lng-shipments-2026-03-04/) | [LNG market disruption: QatarEnergy extends force majeure with Edison](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/01/qatarenergy-extend-force-majeure-september-italys-edison-iran-war-.html).
*   **Price Impact:** European natural gas benchmark prices (TTF) surged by 35% to 54% immediately following the halt [QatarEnergy LNG Market Shift Reshapes Global Energy Security](https://discoveryalert.com.au/qatar-lng-market-2026-geopolitical-dynamics/) | [Gas Prices Surge as Qatar Shuts World’s Largest LNG Export Plant - Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-02/european-gas-rallies-more-than-30-as-qatar-halts-lng-production). Energy prices for glass manufacturing rose by nearly 67% at their peak during the crisis [Hindustan National Glass official says glass industry survived energy shock, but costs and gas supply remain concern- Moneycontrol.com](https://www.moneycontrol.com/news/business/companies/hindustan-national-glass-official-says-glass-industry-survived-energy-shock-but-costs-and-gas-supply-remain-concern-13898914.html).
*   **Surcharges:** Manufacturers rapidly passed costs down the supply chain. In the UK, energy surcharges on single lorry loads of glass rose from £350 to £6,500 (a reported 2,000% increase) in one week in March 2026 [Glass energy surcharges – ‘the cost must go down the supply chain’ | The Glazine](https://www.the-glazine.com/?p=5570) | [2,000 Percent Energy Surcharge Increase Rocks Glass Industry - Window News](https://www.windownews.co.uk/2000-percent-energy-surcharge-increase-rocks-glass-industry/). In the US, Hartung Glass Industries raised its energy surcharge to 16% for West Coast/Mountain West customers in January 2026, and EP Minerals added a $31.50 per short ton surcharge in March 2026 [‘Unprecedented’ Increase in Energy Surcharge Forces Hartung Glass to Adjust Rates](https://www.usglassmag.com/unprecedented-increase-in-energy-surcharge-forces-hartung-glass-to-adjust-rates/) | [Energy Surcharge Policy | EP Minerals](https://epminerals.com/about-us/customer-center/energy-surcharge-policy). Ecolab also announced a global 10-14% energy surcharge effective April 1, 2026 [Ecolab Announces Global 10-14% Energy Surcharge | Ecolab](https://www.ecolab.com/news/2026/03/ecolab-announces-global-10-14-energy-surcharge).
*   **Supply Impact:** Indian glass factories were forced to pare output in April 2026 due to energy shortages, impacting beverage and medical vial supplies [Gas Crunch Guts India Glassmakers Tripping Milk-to-Malt Supply Chains - Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-10/bottles-flasks-grow-scarce-in-india-as-gas-crunch-hits-glassmakers).

### 2. Structural Capacity Reductions and Plant Closures
**Materiality: High** | *Impact: Permanent reduction in global glass packaging capacity*

Faced with subdued demand, high debt, and elevated operating costs, major manufacturers executed aggressive network optimization programs throughout 2025 and 2026. 
*   **O-I Glass:** Realized over $300 million in benefits in 2025 from its 'Fit to Win' program, which involved idling or closing eight furnaces and eliminating approximately 1,500 jobs [O-I GLASS, INC._December 31, 2024](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/812074/000155837025000868/oi-20241231x10k.htm) | [O-I Glass reorienting portfolio to boost non-alcoholic beverages | Packaging Dive](https://www.packagingdive.com/news/oi-glass-q4-2025-packaging-earnings/811848/). Specific closures included its Portland, Oregon plant (August 2025), four Americas furnaces (2025), and halting furnaces in Vergèze and Vayres, France (late 2025/2026) [Major glass end user shuts down Oregon bottling plant - Resource Recycling](https://resource-recycling.com/recycling/2025/08/05/major-glass-end-user-shuts-down-oregon-bottling-plant/) | [O-I Glass to close four furnaces and a plant in Americas](https://www.glass-international.com/news/o-i-glass-to-close-four-furnaces-and-a-plant-in-americas) | [O-I plots short-term Vayres glass closure](https://www.glass-international.com/news/o-i-plots-short-term-vayres-glass-closure).
*   **Ardagh Group:** Facing a $700 million debt obligation in 2025 and a "maturity wall" in 2026, Ardagh closed its Dolton, Illinois plant (February 2025, 316 employees) and its Seattle, Washington facility (January 2026, 245 employees) [Ardagh: Through the Looking Glass - CreditSights](https://know.creditsights.com/insights/ardagh-through-the-looking-glass/) | [Ardagh Glass closing Illinois plant, affecting 316 employees | Packaging Dive](https://www.packagingdive.com/news/ardagh-glass-plant-closure-layoffs-dolton-illinois/739414/) | [Glass, EPS manufacturing hit in November layoff announcements | Packaging Dive](https://www.packagingdive.com/news/november-packaging-manufacturing-closure-layoff-announcements-anchor-ardagh-orora/733976/).
*   **Anchor Hocking:** Closed its 132-year-old Charleroi, Pennsylvania plant, resulting in over 300 layoffs. *(Note: Sources conflict on the exact date, with one citing April 2025 and another citing January 2026)* [Anchor Hocking - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anchor_Hocking) | [Pennsylvania glass plant closure sparks outrage | Manufacturing Dive](https://www.manufacturingdive.com/news/anchor-hocking-charleroi-pennsylvania-glass-pyrex-plant-closure-senators/728703/).
*   **Verallia:** Announced a business review in February 2026 that may result in up to 360 job cuts and potential closures at sites in Essen (Germany), Châteaubernard (France), and Knottingley (UK) [Verallia may cut up to 360 jobs as European glass bottle demand falls | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/verallia-may-cut-up-360-jobs-european-glass-bottle-demand-falls-2026-02-17/) | [Verallia to cut up to 360 jobs](https://www.glass-international.com/news/verallia-to-cut-up-360-jobs).
*   **Other Closures:** Gerresheimer Glass (Chicago Heights, IL by end of FY 2026), Vetropack (Moldova, Dec 2025), Osotspa (Thailand, Feb/Dec 2025), and Nippon Electric Glass (UK, June 2025) [Gerresheimer Glass, Oliver, TriMas, Veritiv Visual announced closures in April | Packaging Dive](https://www.packagingdive.com/news/april-closures-layoffs-clearwater-paper-gerresheimer-glass-graphic-packaging-oliver-trimas/819151/) | [Vetropack starts Moldovan glass furnace closure](https://www.glass-international.com/news/vetropack-starts-moldovan-glass-furnace-closure) | [Glass furnace closure in Thailand](https://www.glass-international.com/news/glass-furnace-closure-in-thailand) | [UK glass plant closure confirmed](https://www.glass-international.com/news/glass-plant-closure-confirmed).

### 3. Trade Policy Volatility and Tariffs
**Materiality: Moderate to High** | *Impact: Disrupted cross-border supply chains, increased landed costs*

*   **US Tariffs:** A baseline 10% tariff on imported goods, including glass packaging, took effect on April 5, 2025, followed by higher reciprocal tariffs on April 9, 2025. The total tariff rate on Chinese glass imports reached 54% to 55%, increasing the estimated landed cost of a standard wine bottle from $1.00 to $2.25 [2025 Tariff Updates: Impact on Packaging Supply Chains](https://evergreenresources.com/2025-packaging-industry-tariffs/) | [US Imposes 34% Tariffs On Chinese Imports - Industry News - News - Migo Glass](https://www.migoglass.com/news/us-imposes-34-tariffs-on-chinese-imports-84978513.html) | [How Trump's 104% Tariff Affects the Glass Bottle Industry?](https://www.crystalglassbottle.com/how-trumps-104-tariff-affects-the-glass-bottle-industry/). 
*   **Retaliation & Reversal:** Canada imposed 25% counter-tariffs on US goods, including float glass, starting March 4, 2025, causing US wine and spirits exports to Canada to plummet by up to 91% [List of products from the United States subject to 25 per cent tariffs effective March 4, 2025](https://www.canada.ca/en/department-finance/news/2025/03/list-of-products-from-the-united-states-subject-to-25-per-cent-tariffs-effective-march-4-2025.html) | [No U.S. Whiskey or Wine? Canada’s Doing Just Fine | Wine Enthusiast](https://www.wineenthusiast.com/culture/industry-news/canadian-whisky-wine-us-tariffs/). In early 2026, the US Supreme Court ruled that the President lacked authority for the broad reciprocal tariffs, leading to duty refunds [Mexico Tariffs 2026: What Manufacturers Actually Need to Know](https://insights.tetakawi.com/mexico-tariffs-2026-what-manufacturers-actually-need-to-know). However, Mexico implemented its own tariff reform on January 1, 2026, raising duties on glass bottles from non-FTA countries to as high as 35% [Mexico Raises Tariffs on Imports from China](https://www.allynintl.com/en/news-publications/entry/mexico-raises-tariffs-on-imports-from-china).

### 4. Freight Disruptions and the "Shortage" Contradiction
**Materiality: Moderate** | *Impact: Extended lead times, increased freight rates*

*   **Logistics Bottlenecks:** The 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis and Red Sea rerouting caused surging freight rates and container shortages. Freight prices for transporting a 40-foot container (FEU) from Asia to the USA held around $5,300 in 2026 [2026 Container Shipping Outlook: Rising Costs, Delays & Solutions](https://www.foursonslogistics.com/blog/2026-container-shipping-costs-outlook/) | [What Awaits Us in 2026 in the Field of Shipping Containers - HZ CONTAINERS.com](https://hz-containers.com/en/news/what-awaits-us-in-2026-in-the-field-of-shipping-containers/). 
*   **Contested Shortage Narrative:** There is a stark contradiction in the market regarding supply availability. B2B suppliers claim standard delivery windows have stretched to several months in 2026, advising brands to maintain 90-day buffers *(Note: Claim sourced from low-confidence B2B marketing material)* [2026 Glass Bottle Supply News Shortages Trends and Solutions - Reihey Glass](https://reihey.com/glass-bottle-supply-news/). Conversely, the Glass Packaging Institute (GPI) explicitly characterized the "glass shortage" as a "false narrative" in late 2025, noting that 12 oz glass container imports were actually up 12% year-to-date [GPI refutes glass shortage claims](https://www.glass-international.com/news/gpi-refutes-glass-shortage-claims) | [Glass Packaging Institute (GPI) Industry Update](https://www.gpi.org/sites/default/files/content-files/West%20Coast%20Protective%20League%20Meeting,%20November%208th,%202025%20-%20GPI%20Deck-2-compressed.pdf). However, the European Container Glass Federation (FEVE) warned in March 2026 of an "unprecedented production decline" across Europe due to energy costs [Industrial Accelerator Act: European Glass Packaging Industry welcomes recognition of glass as a strategic sector - FEVE](https://feve.org/industrial-accelerator-act-european-glass-packaging-industry-welcomes-recognition-of-glass-as-a-strategic-sector/).

### 5. Raw Material Constraints
**Materiality: Low to Moderate** | *Impact: Margin pressure for manufacturers*

*   **Silica Sand:** Global prices increased 14–20% in Q1 2026 due to elevated energy costs, Middle Eastern freight surcharges, and tightening US frac sand supply (which rose 10-12% in Q1 2026) [Silica Sand Market Size, Share & Growth Report, 2035 | CAGR 4.44%](https://www.expertmarketresearch.com/reports/silica-sand-market) | [Silica Sand Price Trend 2026 | Forecast, Data & Analysis](https://www.expertmarketresearch.com/price-forecast/silica-sand-price-trends). Atlantic Coast import delays and diesel trucking surcharges further reduced North American availability in March 2026 [Silica Prices, Trends, Chart, Index and News Q1 2026](https://www.chemanalyst.com/Pricing-data/silica-1304).
*   **Soda Ash:** Despite global prices softening through much of 2025 (falling 19% year-over-year in China), planned maintenance in early 2026 temporarily tightened availability [What’s Next for Soda Ash Prices in Glass 2026?](https://www.chemtradeasia.com/market-insights/soda-ash-prices-glass-manufacturing-2026).

### 6. Labor Disputes and Shortages
**Materiality: Low to Moderate** | *Impact: Localized production halts*

*   **Strikes:** Significant labor actions disrupted production, including a five-month strike by 650 Libbey Glass workers in Ohio (August 2025 to February 2026), an unfair labor practice strike at nine Anchor Glass Container facilities (September 2025), and strikes at Ardagh Glass in the UK in 2025 [New Contract at Libbey Glass - United Steelworkers](https://usw.org/news/new-contract-at-libbey-glass/) | [USW Mould Makers Declare Unfair Labor Practice Strike at Anchor Glass Container - United Steelworkers](https://usw.org/press-release/usw-mould-makers-declare-unfair-labor-practice-strike-at-anchor-glass-container/) | [Ardagh Glass workers to strike in Yorkshire and Ayrshire | glassonweb.com](https://www.glassonweb.com/news/ardagh-glass-workers-strike-yorkshire-and-ayrshire).
*   **Workforce Shortages:** 79% of glass manufacturers reported unfilled positions that are difficult to fill, with over 50% of top players citing recruitment as their most difficult labor challenge in 2025 [What are the biggest challenges facing glass manufacturers today? - Glass Manufacturing Industry Council](https://gmic.org/what-are-the-biggest-challenges-facing-glass-manufacturers-today/) | [Struggling to Keep Production or Field Personnel? | Glass Magazine](https://www.glassmagazine.com/article/struggling-keep-production-or-field-personnel).

---

### Analysis and Risk Assessment

Buyers of glass packaging face three structural risks heading into the second half of 2026:
1.  **Extreme Energy Vulnerability:** The industry's inability to pause furnace operations makes it uniquely susceptible to geopolitical energy shocks. The March 2026 QatarEnergy LNG suspension proved that regional conflicts immediately translate into massive, unavoidable freight and energy surcharges for buyers.
2.  **Consolidated Pricing Power & Antitrust Scrutiny:** The French Competition Authority raided Verallia and Saverglass in March 2025 on suspicion of illegal cartel activity and price coordination [Verallia, Saverglass part of anticompetition investigation in France | Packaging Dive](https://www.packagingdive.com/news/france-glass-raids-verallia-orora-saverglass-antitrust-investigation/743941/). While the Italian Competition Authority cleared manufacturers in January 2026, the UK Trade Remedies Authority launched new investigations into Chinese and Turkish imports in March 2026 [Verallia Acknowledges the Italian Competition Authority’s Decision Confirming Full Compliance With Competition Laws](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/verallia-acknowledges-italian-competition-authority-170000990.html) | [March in review: Iran war disrupts supply chains, regulation updates & Aicomp Summit 2026](https://www.packaginginsights.com/news/march-2026-packaging-update.html). This regulatory environment, paired with massive capacity closures by O-I and Ardagh, leaves buyers with fewer supplier alternatives and high exposure to coordinated price floors.
3.  **Permanent Capacity Loss:** The closures of 2025-2026 are largely permanent structural shifts rather than temporary idlings. Ardagh expects 2026 to be a "transition year" with volume declines before returning to growth in 2027 [Ardagh saw boost for cans, decline for glass in 2025 | Packaging Dive](https://www.packagingdive.com/news/ardagh-packaging-earnings-q4-full-year-2025-results-metal-glass/813311/).

### Gaps and Limitations
*   **Surcharge Specificity:** While major logistics providers and flat glass suppliers confirmed exact energy surcharge percentages, the findings lack explicit confirmation of the exact surcharge percentages applied *directly* to finished glass bottle invoices by the major packaging manufacturers (O-I, Ardagh, Verallia) following the March 2026 crisis.
*   **Date Discrepancies:** Sources conflict on the exact closure date of Anchor Hocking's Charleroi, PA plant (April 2025 vs. January 2026). 
*   **Shortage Severity:** The severity of the 2026 glass bottle "shortage" is highly contested; claims of severe bottlenecks rely heavily on low-confidence B2B marketing sources, which conflict with official statements from the Glass Packaging Institute.

sources.name: Glass furnace closure in Thailand

sources.name: UK glass plant closure confirmed

sources.snippet: Verallia announced a business review in February 2026 that may result in up to 360 job cuts across several European countries, including possible site closures and furnace shutdowns due to subdued demand.

sources.name: Ardagh: Through the Looking Glass - CreditSights
sources.snippet: Ardagh Group faces a $700 million debt obligation due in 2025 and a significant financial 'maturity wall' in 2026, which may impact the company's operating efficiency and asset quality.

sources.name: Ardagh saw boost for cans, decline for glass in 2025 | Packaging Dive
sources.snippet: Ardagh Group expects 2026 to be a 'transition year' with a small volume decline in glass packaging before a projected return to growth in 2027, following cuts to glass production that impacted results.

sources.name: Exclusive: Qatar shuts gas liquefaction, will take weeks to restart, sources say | Reuters
sources.snippet: QatarEnergy declared force majeure on gas exports on March 4, 2026, following drone and missile attacks on its Ras Laffan and Mesaieed facilities during the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

sources.name: ‘Unprecedented’ Increase in Energy Surcharge Forces Hartung Glass to Adjust Rates
sources.snippet: Hartung Glass Industries increased its energy surcharge to 16% for West Coast and Mountain West customers effective January 2026, citing an 'unprecedented increase' of nearly 300% per truckload from flat glass suppliers.

sources.name: Gas Crunch Guts India Glassmakers Tripping Milk-to-Malt Supply Chains - Bloomberg
sources.snippet: Indian glass factories were forced to pare output in April 2026 due to energy shortages resulting from the Gulf war, impacting the supply of beverage bottles and medicine vials.

sources.name: LNG market disruption: QatarEnergy extends force majeure with Edison
sources.snippet: QatarEnergy extended its force majeure notice through early September 2026 for Italy's Edison SpA, withholding a total of 21 LNG cargoes (approximately 2.7 billion cubic meters of gas).

sources.name: Energy Surcharge Policy | EP Minerals
sources.snippet: EP Minerals implemented an energy surcharge of $31.50 per short ton at its Middleton, TN plant effective March 1, 2026, to address natural gas cost fluctuations.

sources.name: QatarEnergy LNG Market Shift Reshapes Global Energy Security
sources.snippet: European natural gas benchmark prices (TTF) rose by 35% to 54% immediately following the March 2026 QatarEnergy production halt.

sources.name: Glass energy surcharges – ‘the cost must go down the supply chain’ | The Glazine
sources.snippet: Glass manufacturers in the UK reported energy surcharges on single lorry loads of glass rising from £350 to £6,500 in the span of one week in March 2026.

sources.name: Ecolab Announces Global 10-14% Energy Surcharge | Ecolab
sources.snippet: Ecolab announced a global 10-14% energy surcharge effective April 1, 2026, citing an 80% rise in natural gas prices in Europe and a 60% increase in oil prices since late 2025.

sources.name: Silica Sand Market Size, Share & Growth Report, 2035 | CAGR 4.44%
sources.snippet: Global silica sand prices experienced a 14–20% increase in Q1 2026, driven by elevated energy costs, freight surcharges on Middle Eastern trade routes, and tightening US frac sand supply.

sources.name: Silica Sand Price Trend 2026 | Forecast, Data & Analysis
sources.snippet: North American silica sand prices fell 18.30% cumulatively in 2025 to USD 37.86/MT, driven by a surplus of frac sand production that overwhelmed declining drilling activity.

sources.name: Silica Prices, Trends, Chart, Index and News Q1 2026
sources.snippet: In March 2026, Atlantic Coast import delays and diesel trucking surcharges reduced silica sand availability in North America, tightening regional supply and increasing spot prices.

sources.name: Silica Sand Price Trend 2026 | Forecast, Data & Analysis
sources.snippet: US frac sand demand rose approximately 10-12% in Q1 2026 compared to Q4 2025, tightening domestic supply across all industrial silica sand grades.

sources.name: Ardagh Glass workers to strike in Yorkshire and Ayrshire | glassonweb.com
sources.snippet: Workers at Ardagh Glass in Yorkshire and Ayrshire went on strike in 2025 after rejecting a 5.5% pay rise offer over two years.

sources.name: USW Mould Makers Declare Unfair Labor Practice Strike at Anchor Glass Container - United Steelworkers
sources.snippet: United Steelworker (USW) members at nine Anchor Glass Container facilities launched an unfair labor practice strike at 12:01 a.m. on September 1, 2025, over issues including wages, pensions, and healthcare.

sources.name: Glass, EPS manufacturing hit in November layoff announcements | Packaging Dive
sources.snippet: Ardagh Glass permanently closed its Seattle glass production facility, which serviced wine customers, with layoffs for 245 workers beginning on November 7, 2025.

sources.name: Ardagh saw boost for cans, decline for glass in 2025 | Packaging Dive
sources.snippet: Ardagh facilities and customer shipments faced disruptions in January 2026 due to a 'challenging metal supply chain situation' and severe winter weather.

sources.name: What are the biggest challenges facing glass manufacturers today? - Glass Manufacturing Industry Council
sources.snippet: Glass industry leaders reported in 2025 and 2026 that labor shortages are the 'worst news' for the industry, with 79% of manufacturers reporting unfilled positions that are difficult to fill.

sources.name: Struggling to Keep Production or Field Personnel? | Glass Magazine
sources.snippet: In Spring 2025, more than 50% of top glass industry players cited recruitment as their most difficult labor challenge, specifically for entry-level and skilled production positions.

sources.name: New Contract at Libbey Glass - United Steelworkers
sources.snippet: Libbey Glass workers in Toledo, Ohio, conducted a five-month unfair labor practice strike involving 650 workers that began in August 2025 and ended with a contract ratification in February 2026.

sources.name: Glass, EPS manufacturing hit in November layoff announcements | Packaging Dive
sources.snippet: Anchor Glass was identified as one of the companies making downsizing and closure announcements across Washington, Florida, California, Pennsylvania, and Tennessee in late 2025.

sources.name: 2026 Glass Bottle Supply News Shortages Trends and Solutions - Reihey Glass
sources.snippet: A strike at a major glass manufacturing plant in the United Kingdom in early 2026 has sparked significant concerns regarding a glass bottle shortage for beverage brands.

sources.name: 2026 Glass Bottle Supply News Shortages Trends and Solutions - Reihey Glass
sources.snippet: Standard delivery windows for finished glass bottles have stretched from a few weeks to several months in 2026, paralyzing agile supply chains for beverage and cosmetic brands.

sources.name: 2026 Container Shipping Outlook: Rising Costs, Delays & Solutions
sources.snippet: Container shipping costs in 2026 are rising due to Red Sea rerouting, port congestion, and global reroutes around the Cape of Good Hope, impacting the reliability of glass bottle deliveries.

sources.name: What Awaits Us in 2026 in the Field of Shipping Containers - HZ CONTAINERS.com
sources.snippet: In 2026, freight prices for transporting a 40-foot container (FEU) from Asia to the USA are holding around $5,300, while routes to Europe are reaching the $4,500–$5,500 range.

sources.name: 2026 Glass Bottle Supply News Shortages Trends and Solutions - Reihey Glass
sources.snippet: Beverage brands are being advised to maintain at least a 90-day buffer of custom glass bottles and order bulk inventory 3 to 6 months in advance to defend against sudden shipping bottlenecks in 2026.

sources.name: What Awaits Us in 2026 in the Field of Shipping Containers - HZ CONTAINERS.com
sources.snippet: The shift of manufacturing from China to Mexico, India, and Vietnam is increasing pressure on port logistics in those regions, leading to greater strain on Mexican infrastructure for goods moving to the USA in 2026.

sources.name: GPI refutes glass shortage claims
sources.snippet: Scott DeFife, President of the Glass Packaging Institute (GPI), characterizes the 'glass shortage' as a 'false narrative,' stating that while some end-market customers may have issues obtaining bottle stock, it does not constitute a systemic glass shortage.

sources.name: Industrial Accelerator Act: European Glass Packaging Industry welcomes recognition of glass as a strategic sector - FEVE
sources.snippet: The European Container Glass Federation (FEVE) reported in March 2026 that the industry is facing 'unprecedented production decline and furnaces closures across Europe' due to high energy and carbon costs and intense global competition.

sources.name: 2026 Glass Bottle Supply News Shortages Trends and Solutions - Reihey Glass
sources.snippet: A strike at a large glass factory in the UK in 2026 sparked specific concerns regarding a localized bottle shortage.

sources.name: Glass Packaging Institute (GPI) Industry Update
sources.snippet: As of November 2025, GPI reported that 12 oz glass container imports were up 12% year-to-date, while 750ml bottle imports were down 8% across all countries except Mexico.

sources.name: Gas Prices Surge as Qatar Shuts World’s Largest LNG Export Plant - Bloomberg
sources.snippet: European benchmark gas prices (Title Transfer Facility) surged by as much as 54% in early March 2026 immediately following the halt of Qatari LNG production.

sources.name: Ecolab Announces Global 10-14% Energy Surcharge | Ecolab
sources.snippet: Ecolab implemented a global 10-14% energy surcharge effective April 1, 2026, to manage extraordinary cost pressures during the crisis.

sources.name: Verallia, Saverglass part of anticompetition investigation in France | Packaging Dive
sources.snippet: The French Competition Authority (Autorité de la concurrence) conducted unannounced raids and seizures at Verallia's headquarters in a Paris suburb on March 27, 2025.

sources.name: Verallia, Saverglass part of anticompetition investigation in France | Packaging Dive
sources.snippet: Orora Limited's Saverglass business headquarters in northern France were raided by the French Competition Authority on March 27, 2025.

sources.name: Verallia, Saverglass part of anticompetition investigation in France | Packaging Dive
sources.snippet: The French antitrust investigation was prompted by a complaint from beverage industry participants regarding price increases in the glass packaging sector.

sources.name: Verallia Acknowledges the Italian Competition Authority’s Decision Confirming Full Compliance With Competition Laws
sources.snippet: On January 8, 2026, the Italian Competition Authority (AGCM) closed a separate investigation into several glass manufacturers, including Verallia Italia, without finding any infringement.

sources.name: March in review: Iran war disrupts supply chains, regulation updates & Aicomp Summit 2026
sources.snippet: In March 2026, the UK Trade Remedies Authority (TRA) launched two investigations into glass container imports from China and Türkiye to examine if unfair competition is harming the UK glass industry.

sources.name: 2025 Tariff Updates: Impact on Packaging Supply Chains
sources.snippet: A baseline 10% tariff on all imported goods, including glass packaging, took effect on April 5, 2025, followed by higher 'reciprocal tariffs' on April 9, 2025, for countries with large trade deficits.

sources.name: US Imposes 34% Tariffs On Chinese Imports - Industry News - News - Migo Glass
sources.snippet: The total tariff rate on Chinese glass imports reached 54% to 55% in 2025, combining a new 34% levy effective April 9 with existing 20% duties.

sources.name: How Trump's 104% Tariff Affects the Glass Bottle Industry?
sources.snippet: The landed cost of a standard wine bottle was projected to increase from $1.00 to $2.25 after the application of the 2025 tariffs.

sources.name: No U.S. Whiskey or Wine? Canada’s Doing Just Fine | Wine Enthusiast
sources.snippet: U.S. wine exports to Canada fell by 80-91% in 2025, and spirits exports dropped by 63-85%, following the imposition of 25% tariffs and subsequent Canadian retaliatory measures.

sources.name: Mexico Tariffs 2026: What Manufacturers Actually Need to Know
sources.snippet: The U.S. Supreme Court ruled in early 2026 that the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) did not authorize the President to impose the broad reciprocal tariffs enacted in April 2025, leading to duty refunds for many importers.

sources.name: Mexico Raises Tariffs on Imports from China
sources.snippet: Mexico implemented its own tariff reform effective January 1, 2026, raising duties on glass bottles and jars from non-FTA countries (primarily China) to as high as 35%.

sources.name: List of products from the United States subject to 25 per cent tariffs effective March 4, 2025
sources.snippet: Canada imposed 25% counter-tariffs on $30 billion of U.S. goods starting March 4, 2025, which included float glass and other packaging materials used by the beverage industry.
